Title: A novel synergistic regulatory mechanism involving the MYB39-MYB111-bHLH51-TTG1 module in the phenolic and diterpenoid biosynthetic pathways of Salvia miltiorrhiza

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with JA-induced accumulation of phenolic acids and tanshinones; interacts with SmMYB39 and is proposed to relieve SmMYB39 interference on the SmMBW complex, but direct metabolite-function validation in this paper is not established.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: high-throughput Y2H screening; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; MeJA-induction expression analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript significantly upregulated at nearly all time points after 100 μM MeJA treatment in hairy roots.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmJAZ1 was identified as an SmMYB39-interacting protein by Y2H library screening and confirmed by Y2H, BiFC and Co-IP. 2. SmJAZ1 transcript was induced by MeJA in hairy roots. 3. The paper proposes SmJAZ1 may interfere with SmMYB39 and thereby favor SmMBW complex function in JA-responsive phenolic acid and tanshinone accumulation, but this mechanism remains speculative.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion cites previous work that SmJAZ1 is an activator of JA-induced RA and tanshinone biosynthesis, but in this paper no direct overexpression/silencing metabolite assay for SmJAZ1 was reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential JA-signaling engineering target for coordinated enhancement of phenolic acids and tanshinones, but requires direct functional validation before deployment.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: No public omics accession; high-throughput yeast two-hybrid library screening reported.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenolic acids and tanshinones are medicinal secondary metabolites in S. miltiorrhiza.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: rosmarinic acid (RA); salvianolic acid B (SAB); cryptotanshinone (CT); dihydrotanshinone I (DT-I); tanshinone I (T-I); tanshinone IIA (T-IIA)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal secondary metabolites of S. miltiorrhiza.
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; subgroup 4 MYB repressor; MBW interferer-like regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subgroup 4 R2R3-MYB with conserved repressor motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of phenolic acid and tanshinone biosynthesis via repression of pathway genes and inhibition of MBW complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression; protein–protein interaction-mediated interference with MBW complex formation; inhibition of SmbHLH51 transactivation activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator coordinating suppression of both phenolic acid and diterpenoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression markedly decreases RA, SAB, CT, T-I, DT-I and T-IIA; RNAi increases both phenolic acids and tanshinones.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy-root overexpression; RNAi; HPLC metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR of pathway genes; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; LCI; Dual-LUC; MeJA-induction expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Indirectly represses multiple pathway genes including SmPAL1; SmC4H1; Sm4CL2; SmTAT; SmHPPR; SmDXS2; SmDXR; SmHMGR1; SmGGPPS; SmCPS1; SmKSL1; inhibits SmbHLH51 activation of SmC4H1 and SmKSL1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Downregulates key phenolic-acid and tanshinone biosynthetic genes; reduces SmbHLH51-mediated activation of SmC4H1 and SmKSL1 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with all components of the positive SmMYB111-SmbHLH51-SmTTG1 MBW complex and interferes with its formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signaling; exogenous MeJA suppresses its transcript accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript significantly reduced at each time point after 100 μM MeJA treatment in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMYB39 negatively regulates both phenolic acid and tanshinone accumulation in hairy roots. 2. SmMYB39 represses expression of multiple biosynthetic genes in both pathways. 3. SmMYB39 physically interacts with SmMYB111, SmbHLH51 and SmTTG1 and interferes with MBW complex formation. 4. SmMYB39 suppresses SmbHLH51 transactivation of SmC4H1 and SmKSL1. 5. SmMYB39 is repressed by MeJA and interacts with SmJAZ1, linking this module to JA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose SmMYB39 acts as an MBW interferer (MBWi).
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Silencing/editing SmMYB39 is a promising strategy to simultaneously elevate medicinal phenolic acids and tanshinones in S. miltiorrhiza.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: No public accession; high-throughput Y2H library screening reported.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: rosmarinic acid (RA); salvianolic acid B (SAB); cryptotanshinone (CT); dihydrotanshinone I (DT-I); tanshinone I (T-I); tanshinone IIA (T-IIA)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal secondary metabolites of S. miltiorrhiza.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Positive transcriptional regulation of phenolic acid and tanshinone biosynthesis as MBW complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation in cooperation with SmbHLH51 and SmTTG1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator promoting accumulation of both pathways' metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases RA, SAB and multiple tanshinones; gene editing/silencing decreases them.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy-root overexpression; gene editing/silencing; HPLC metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; Y2H/LCI interaction evidence with SmbHLH51 and SmTTG1; MeJA-induction expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Promotes expression of pathway genes including SmPAL1; SmC4H1; Sm4CL2; SmRAS; SmCPS1; SmHMGR1; SmKSL1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of multiple phenolic-acid and tanshinone pathway genes, likely through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core MYB component of the positive SmMYB111-SmbHLH51-SmTTG1 MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript significantly upregulated at nearly all time points after 100 μM MeJA treatment in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMYB111 positively regulates both phenolic acids and tanshinones in hairy roots. 2. Overexpression upregulates multiple biosynthetic genes, while loss/silencing reduces them. 3. SmMYB111 interacts with SmbHLH51 and SmTTG1 as part of an MBW complex. 4. SmMYB39 antagonizes its complex formation. 5. SmMYB111 is induced by MeJA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported as a positive regulator of phenolic acids; this study extends function to tanshinones.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful activator for co-enhancing phenolic acids and tanshinones, especially in combination with other MBW components or SmMYB39 suppression.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: rosmarinic acid (RA); salvianolic acid B (SAB); cryptotanshinone (CT); dihydrotanshinone I (DT-I); tanshinone I (T-I); tanshinone IIA (T-IIA)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal secondary metabolites of S. miltiorrhiza.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Direct transcriptional activation of phenolic acid and tanshinone biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct promoter binding and transcriptional activation; functions within MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator linking the MBW module to structural genes in both pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases phenolic acids and tanshinones; RNAi decreases them.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy-root overexpression; RNAi; HPLC metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; Dual-LUC; Y2H/BiFC/Co-IP/LCI interaction assays; MeJA-induction expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds and activates SmC4H1 and SmKSL1 promoters; also elevates broader pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: bHLH component of the positive SmMYB111-SmbHLH51-SmTTG1 MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MeJA induces transcript accumulation; activity is antagonized by SmMYB39
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript significantly upregulated at nearly all time points after 100 μM MeJA treatment in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmbHLH51 positively regulates both phenolic acid and tanshinone biosynthesis in hairy roots. 2. It directly binds G-box-containing promoters of SmC4H1 and SmKSL1. 3. It activates SmC4H1 and SmKSL1 transcription in Dual-LUC assays. 4. It functions in the SmMBW complex with SmMYB111 and SmTTG1. 5. SmMYB39 interacts with SmbHLH51 and represses its transactivation activity. 6. SmbHLH51 is induced by MeJA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among tested promoters, direct binding was confirmed for SmC4H1 and SmKSL1 but not for SmTAT, SmHPPR, SmDXS1, SmDXR, SmCPS1 and SmCYP76AH1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for simultaneous enhancement of phenolic acids and tanshinones via direct activation of gateway genes.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: rosmarinic acid (RA); salvianolic acid B (SAB); cryptotanshinone (CT); dihydrotanshinone I (DT-I); tanshinone I (T-I); tanshinone IIA (T-IIA)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal secondary metabolites of S. miltiorrhiza.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Positive regulation of phenolic acid and tanshinone biosynthesis as scaffold/cofactor of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive MBW component required for strong accumulation of both metabolite classes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases RA, SAB and multiple tanshinones; gene editing reduces them.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hairy-root overexpression; gene editing; HPLC metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; Y2H/BiFC/Co-IP/LCI interaction assays; MeJA-induction expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Indirectly promotes pathway genes including SmPAL1; SmC4H1; SmTAT1; SmHPPR; SmRAS; SmCYP98A14; SmHMGR1; SmKSL1; SmCPS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances expression of multiple phenolic-acid and tanshinone biosynthetic genes through MBW complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: WD40 component of the positive SmMYB111-SmbHLH51-SmTTG1 MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript significantly upregulated at nearly all time points after 100 μM MeJA treatment in hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in transgenic hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmTTG1 positively regulates phenolic acid and tanshinone accumulation in hairy roots. 2. Overexpression strongly increases pathway-gene expression and metabolite levels, whereas gene editing decreases them. 3. SmTTG1 functions as a component of the SmMBW complex with SmMYB111 and SmbHLH51. 4. SmMYB39 interacts with SmTTG1 and interferes with MBW complex formation. 5. SmTTG1 is induced by MeJA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct DNA binding was not tested; role is inferred as MBW complex cofactor/scaffold.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful MBW component for coordinated metabolic engineering of phenolic acids and tanshinones, especially in combination with SmMYB111/SmbHLH51.
